meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
items.summary: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

items.headline: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
items.summary: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new…

items.headline: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
items.summary: Anthropic's latest advert is stirring up high emotions — which is undoubtedly what it was designed to do.

items.headline: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
items.summary: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

items.headline: SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
items.summary: SpaceXAI's Grok Build AI coding tool was spotted uploading users' entire codebases to Google Cloud before it was reported, and the company turned it off. The Register reports that Cereblab published findings on Monday showing how the Grok…

items.headline: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
items.summary: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

items.headline: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
items.summary: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.

items.headline: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
items.summary: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

items.headline: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
items.summary: A group of 26 former Meta employees is suing the company over claims that it used AI tools to unfairly target workers on leave with layoffs, as reported earlier by Reuters. In the lawsuit, the employees allege Meta determined which workers…

items.headline: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
items.summary: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.

items.headline: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
items.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI…

items.headline: The Google Images homepage will recommend photos even before you search
items.summary: Google is announcing a big change to the Google Images homepage in honor of the platform's 25th anniversary this week. Instead of a mostly blank page with a search bar, the homepage will soon show you a bunch of images that it thinks you…

items.headline: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
items.summary: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

items.headline: Spotify is now an AI chatbot, too
items.summary: Spotify is experimenting with a new AI feature that allows Premium subscribers to play and explore music, audiobooks, and podcasts by having conversations with a chatbot. The "Talk to Spotify" feature appears across the Home and Now…

items.headline: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
items.summary: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

items.headline: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
items.summary: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.

items.headline: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
items.summary: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

items.headline: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

items.headline: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
items.summary: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

items.headline: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit
items.summary: OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world's richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile legal actions yet - from Apple. OpenAI's…

items.headline: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
items.summary: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.

items.headline: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced…

items.headline: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
items.summary: Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog with the power to hit the brakes if frontier models become too dangerous. Writing in a blog post, the Google DeepMind CEO and cofounder said the US should lead the initiative, arguing…

items.headline: How to manage AI investments in the agentic era
items.summary: Learn how enterprises can manage AI investments in the agentic era by measuring useful work per dollar, improving efficiency, and scaling high-value workflows.
